relevance_summary: BPGM is implicated in the pathophysiology of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) and may serve as a potential biomarker for early detection.

relevance_summary: PIP5K1B is a candidate biomarker for early detection of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) due to its role in lipid metabolism and cellular signaling.
citations: Discovery of lipid metabolism-related diagnostic biomarkers and construction of diagnostic model in steroid-induced osteonecrosis of femoral head. (2025). https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40823179/

relevance_summary: TMCC2 is a potential biomarker for early detection of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) due to its involvement in immune-related processes identified in peripheral blood analyses.
citations: Screening of Potential Biomarkers in the Peripheral Serum for Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head Based on WGCNA and Machine Learning Algorithms. (2022). https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35154510/

relevance_summary: TSTA3 is a potential diagnostic biomarker for Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), linked to immune cell infiltration and early detection.
citations: ARG2, MAP4K5 and TSTA3 as Diagnostic Markers of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head and Their Correlation With Immune Infiltration. (2021). https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34381494/

relevance_summary: HEMGN is identified as a potential diagnostic biomarker for Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) due to its downregulation in affected patients.
citations: HEMGN and SLC2A1 might be potential diagnostic biomarkers of steroid-induced osteonecrosis of femoral head: study based on WGCNA and DEGs screening. (2021). https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33451334/

relevance_summary: GYPA is a potential biomarker for early detection of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) due to its altered expression in affected patients.
